frame_analyses.description: Initial setup view showing FLS trainer with suture, needle driver, and scissors visible. Penrose drain with marks visible on training surface.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good initial setup with all necessary instruments positioned

frame_analyses.description: Trainee beginning to load needle onto needle driver. Both instruments visible and positioned for needle loading.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper instrument positioning for needle loading phase

frame_analyses.description: Needle loading continues with needle driver grasping the suture needle. Positioning appears appropriate for subsequent suture placement.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grip positioning on needle driver

frame_analyses.description: Trainee positioning needle for first pass through the Penrose drain. Target marks are visible on the drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Approaching first suture placement site with controlled movements

frame_analyses.description: Needle being passed through the drain at the first mark. Suture placement appears to be progressing appropriately.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First needle pass execution looks well-controlled

frame_analyses.description: Continuing suture placement process, working toward second mark on the drain. Suture material visible extending from first placement.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good progression toward completing suture placement through both marks

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot tying process with first throw. Suture ends being manipulated for surgeon's knot formation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initiating knot tying sequence with appropriate suture length

frame_analyses.description: First throw (surgeon's knot) being formed and tightened. Double wrap visible consistent with surgeon's knot technique.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper surgeon's knot formation with double throw visible

frame_analyses.description: Transitioning to second throw with hand switching. Single throw being formed in opposite direction to first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate hand switching between throws, maintaining knot security

frame_analyses.description: Black screen with Spanish text overlay - appears to be title/credit screen interrupting video.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Video interruption - not part of task performance

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double wrap visible
Single throw with proper hand switching
Final single throw completing knot sequence

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain appears well-approximated with no visible gap after knot tightening

technique_summary: Video shows incomplete task performance due to significant interruption with title screen overlay covering majority of footage. From visible portions, technique appears competent with proper knot sequence.
improvement_suggestions: Ensure uninterrupted video recording for complete assessment
Focus on more precise suture placement to minimize deviation from marks
strengths: Good instrument handling in visible portions
